Berthing Update
We are Nigeria's
No. 1 PORT OPERATIONS COMPANY

Sea port terminal in West Africa

Our Business Mission Our Vision

"To become a reference point as a multi-industry global business player where our people are our greatest assets and our market our greatest resource"
"To create a strong and well trusted Nigerian owned corporate entity with diverse business involvements, that will become a pedestal to showcase Nigerians as effective, forthright and exceedingly capable global business players"
  • Open Stacking Area
    (Sq.Meters)
  • Our Reports
  • Container Stacking
    (Sq.Meters)
  • Equipment Profile
  • Human Capacity
  • Warehouse Space
    (Sq.Meters)
watch the tour video

  • ENL currently runs the terminals C and D of the Apapa Port Complex in Lagos. We are the largest indigenous sea port in the country and we offer professional services with integrity and best practice.

  • ENL Haulage boasts of new fleets of trucks. These trucks include the Flat bed with side drop downs and low loaders. They have been made available to assist with transportation of Cargoes to and from the Terminal. The trucks ply both local and up-country.Our Haulage system includes a tracking system for monitoring of the Trucks Cargo and Location.

  • Our Cargo handling operations is at international standard and promotes global consistency and safety. We employ best practice processes and procedure.

  • We have large storage capacity for all Goods and Cargoes.

  • ENL offers Safety, Security and Proper Management of all Cargoes in our custody. We adhere to industry standards and best practice.
    You can rely on us to keep your goods, safe and secure.

  • ENL offers Safety, Security and Proper Management of all Cargoes in our custody. We adhere to industry standards and best practice.
    You can rely on us to keep your goods, safe and secure.

latest Update

register for newsletter

get latest company news

ENL Consortium Ltd. was founded in Nigeria in 2002 primarily to bring together expertise in utility management as detailed above. The consortiums founder, Prince C. A. Haastrup had a vision and purpose to expand the activities of ENL to actively embrace the privatization initiatives of the Government of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

To this end he engaged the management team of ENL to pursue the proposed privatization of the Ports Sector, having already successfully been awarded contracts in the Electricity sector.
